visual_features.overall_style: Conservative, corporate-academic style with a very simple, text-centric layout. Dark header band, white content area, and minimal decorative elements. The look feels slightly dated (pre-material / pre-flat 2.0) but still clean and functional.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven mainly by size, weight, and color contrast. The black header strip with logo and navigation is the strongest visual band. Within the page content, the 'Jobs' heading is the main focal point (larger, bold, darker gray), followed by the red-highlighted email addresses. Numbered lines of text are secondary and given extra separation to read as distinct points.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Very generous vertical whitespace. The main content column has large top margin under the navigation and wide gaps between numbered items and paragraphs, giving an airy, low-density feel. Horizontal whitespace is also generous: the content is constrained to a fairly narrow centered column on a full-width white canvas, creating broad side margins.
unique_elements: Large script-style logotype ('Interbit Research') combined with a circular emblem (open book) on a dark header background
Dual-header structure: a thin top info bar with phone number and a thicker primary navigation bar below
Navigation item using a solid red background to indicate the active section ('Careers')
Cluster of small, square social media icons on the right side of the header

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column or similar desktop grid with a fixed-width centered content column; header uses a full-width horizontal grid subdivided into logo, navigation menu, and social/search areas.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width horizontal header bands with a single centered content column below. No sidebars; simple top-to-bottom, single-column layout.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top info bar (phone) → brand/logo + primary navigation + social/search → large white content area with a single section 'Jobs' that contains heading + brief intro + numbered responsibilities + closing call-to-action paragraph.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header elements are horizontally aligned: logo left, navigation centered, utilities right. Main content is center-aligned text inside a centered column, making the page very symmetrical. Text lines themselves are center-aligned, not left-aligned, which reinforces the formal, announcement-like feel.
responsive_hints: Header elements are arranged in a way that suggests they could stack (top info bar, then logo/nav, then social/search) on smaller breakpoints.
Navigation items are evenly spaced horizontally, implying a flex or inline-block layout that could be collapsed into a mobile menu on narrow screens.
Content area is a single narrow column that should scale well on mobile with minimal changes (font-size and spacing adjustments).
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. A thin info bar spans the full width at the very top. Directly beneath is a thicker navbar containing the logo on the far left, horizontal menu links centered, and social icons plus a search icon on the right. Below the header, a large expanse of white leads into a centered 'Jobs' heading. Beneath it, center-aligned text describes activities in a numbered list format (each number on its own line, separated by generous vertical margins). A final center-aligned paragraph with red email addresses closes the page content.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The top header area (phone bar + navigation) occupies roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport height on a typical desktop. The remainder is dominated by white space and a short content block centered vertically within it. The text block itself only uses a relatively small vertical slice; the main page impression is of a large white canvas with a compact center cluster of text. Side margins are wide due to the narrow text column, giving the content central prominence.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Very low content density. There is a small amount of text relative to the available space, with large margins and line breaks used liberally. This produces a calm, uncluttered experience, but it also results in some unused, visually empty regions.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye first catches the high-contrast black header and logo on the left, then scans horizontally across the navigation and social icons (roughly a top horizontal line of a Z-pattern). From there, the strong contrast between black header and white content draws the eye downward to the center where the 'Jobs' heading sits. Subsequent reading is a straightforward vertical flow down the numbered items, ending on the red-highlighted email addresses which stand out as the final call-to-action.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary interactive treatment is exemplified by the active navigation item 'Careers': a sharp-cornered rectangular tab with solid fill (#D6453A), no visible border, and white text in a medium weight. Height is modest (approx. 32–40px) with horizontal padding around 16–24px, giving it a compact pill-like footprint but with square corners. Other nav items are text-only on a dark background, relying on color (red) to distinguish the active/primary state.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as <ul><li><a> items inside a nav bar, with the active item styled via a class (e.g., .active) applying background-color, padding, and text color. Could also be reused for primary CTAs elsewhere as <a class="btn"> or <button> elements sharing the same class names.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit card components are present in this specific view. If cards were introduced, they would logically follow the site's aesthetic: white rectangles with subtle gray borders (#E0E0E0) or a very faint shadow, sharp or slightly rounded corners (2–4px radius), and generous internal padding (16–24px). Headings would be in dark gray with body text in standard gray.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure: a wrapping <div class="card"> containing an optional <h3> or <h4> for the card title, followed by paragraphs or lists. For conference or project listings, an image or icon could sit at the top, followed by textual details, arranged via flex or block layout.

ui_patterns.visual_description: There is no form visible in the screenshot, but consistent styling would likely use full-width text inputs with sharp or slightly rounded corners, a light gray border (#CCCCCC), and white background. Labels would be above the inputs, left-aligned, with medium spacing (4–8px) between label and field. Error/emphasis text would likely use the site's red accent for messages.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical stacked form layout: <form> with <div class="form-group"> wrappers, each containing a <label> above an <input> or <textarea>. Submit buttons could reuse the primary button style (red background, white text). Validation messages would appear below inputs as small text spans.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal main navigation in a dark bar. Menu items are white text, evenly spaced, likely uppercase or title case, with moderate letter spacing. The active item uses a solid red background while others sit directly on the dark header background. Some items ('Who we are', 'Careers', 'Conferences') display small downward carets indicating dropdown menus. Social icons on the right are square buttons with dark gray backgrounds and white platform icons. A magnifying glass icon at the far right suggests a search trigger.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implementation likely uses <nav><ul><li><a> for the primary menu, with nested <ul> inside <li> elements for dropdowns. Social icons are likely an inline list or flex row of <a> elements containing icon fonts (e.g., <i class="fa fa-facebook">) or SVGs. The search icon may be a button or link that triggers an overlay or expands a search input.

list.visual_description: Within the content, the responsibilities are presented as a simple numbered sequence but typed as separate lines with explicit numbers ("1.", "2." etc.) rather than a visually styled ordered list. Each line is center-aligned and given ample vertical spacing, which makes them read as discrete statements rather than a compact list. No bullet icons or separators are visible.
